title: Occupations
description: Learn occupations in Filipino Sign Language

pages.title: Welcome to Occupations
pages.content: In this lesson, you'll learn how to sign occupations in Filipino Sign Language (FSL). You'll learn 9 important signs with step-by-step instructions and practice questions.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Student'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Student'. A person who is studying at a school or college. 

How to sign:
1. Sign LEARN: dominant '5' hand takes knowledge from the non-dominant palm and moves it to the forehead. 2. Sign AGENT ('person'): both flat hands move straight down parallel.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Doctor/Nurse'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Doctor/Nurse'. A person qualified to practice medicine/A person trained to care for the sick. 

How to sign:
1. Sign DOCTOR: Dominant 'D' handshape taps the wrist (taking a pulse). OR Sign NURSE: Dominant 'N' handshape taps the wrist.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Teacher'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Teacher'. A person who teaches. 

How to sign:
1. Sign TEACH: Both 'flat O' handshapes start near the sides of the head and move forward simultaneously. 2. Sign AGENT ('person'): both flat hands move straight down parallel.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Police Officer'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Police Officer'. A member of the civil force responsible for law enforcement. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'C' handshape. 2. Tap the chest where a badge would be.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Firefighter'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Firefighter'. A person who fights fires. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'B' handshape held at the side of the head (simulating a helmet strap). 2. Sign AGENT ('person'): both flat hands move straight down parallel.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Soldier'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Soldier'. A person who serves in an army. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'A' handshape. 2. Tap the shoulder twice (simulating an epaulet or salute).

pages.title: How to Sign 'Mayor/Council Member'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Mayor/Council Member'. An elected head of a city/A member of a governing council. 

How to sign:
1. Sign MAYOR: Dominant 'M' handshape taps the opposite shoulder twice. 2. Sign AGENT ('person'): both flat hands move straight down parallel.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Social Worker'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Social Worker'. A person engaged in social work. 

How to sign:
1. Sign SOCIAL: Dominant 'S' hand circles forward. 2. Sign WORK: Dominant wrist taps the non-dominant wrist twice. 3. Sign AGENT ('person').

pages.title: How to Sign 'Librarian'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Librarian'. A person who works in a library. 

How to sign:
1. Sign LIBRARY: Dominant 'L' handshape moves in a clockwise circle. 2. Sign AGENT ('person'): both flat hands move straight down parallel.

pages.title: Practice: Student
pages.content: Test your understanding with this question.

question.explanation:
This sign means 'Student'. A person who is studying at a school or college.

How to sign: 1. Sign LEARN: dominant '5' hand takes knowledge from the non-dominant palm and moves it to the forehead. 2. Sign AGENT ('person'): both flat hands move straight down parallel.

pages.title: Practice: Teacher
pages.content: Test your understanding with this question.

question.explanation:
This sign means 'Teacher'. A person who teaches.

How to sign: 1. Sign TEACH: Both 'flat O' handshapes start near the sides of the head and move forward simultaneously. 2. Sign AGENT ('person'): both flat hands move straight down parallel.
